MINI MINUTES FOR DEC-29-2016 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF AMANDA LAMB'S MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER GOVERNING DISCOVERY. A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED IN SUBSTANTIAL PART. THE COURT AGREES IN LARGE PART WITH THE RECOMMENDATION AND RATIONALE OF THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE DRAFT PROTECTIVE ORDER PREPARED BY DEFENDANTS SHOULD INCLUDE LANGUAGE THAT ALLOWS FOR THE SHARING OF INFORMATION MARKED CONFIDENTIAL TO COUNSEL FOR SIMILARLY SITUATED PARTIES IN PENDING LITIGATION AND THAT THE NOTICE NEEDS TO INCLUDE THE NAME OF THE COUNSEL AND A COPY OF THE COMPLAINT IN THE OTHER CASE SUBJECT TO THE FOLLOWING PROTECTIONS: 1) THE RECIPIENT OF SUCH CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION SHALL ACKNOWLEDGE IN WRITING THAT HE/SHE IS NOT PRESENTLY NOR INTENDS TO BE A COMPETITOR OR EMPLOYED BY A COMPETITOR OF ANY OF THE PARTIES OR COUNSEL FOR ANY SUCH PERSON, AND WILL NOT USE ANY CONFIDENTIAL DOCUMENT, TRANSCRIPT OR INFORMATION OBTAINED FROM ANY CONFIDENTIAL DOCUMENT OR TRANSCRIPT TO COMPETE WITH ANY OF THE PARTIES; AND 2) PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL MAY NOT MAKE SUCH A DISCLOSURE WITHOUT PROVIDING 30 DAYS ADVANCE NOTIFICATION OF THE INTENT TO MAKE SUCH DISCLOSURE TO DEFENDANTS' COUNSEL AND DURING THAT 30 DAY PERIOD DEFENDANTS HAVE NOT FILED A MOTION FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER PROHIBITING THE DISCLOSURE. THIS ORDER WILL BE EFFECTIVE THREE YEARS OR AT THE END OF THE CASE, WHICHEVER COMES LATER. PARTIES TO MEET AND CONFER REGARDING FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR 5976

Notice Of Plaintiffs Motion For Protective Order Governing Discovery?
Off Calendar Dec-29-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. VALERIE N. ROSE OF THE LAW OFFICES OF WALKUP, MELODIA KELLY & SCHOENBERGER, TEL. NO. 1-415-981-7210 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. ROBERT TAITZ OF TYSON & MENDES LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 1-415-464-4935 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC.

RULING - A R G U E D; The court modified its tentative ruling and ruled as follows: Plaintiff Amanda Lamb's motion for protective order governing discovery is granted in substantial part. The court agrees in large part with the recommendation and rationale of the judge pro tem. The draft protective order prepared by defendants should include language that allows for the sharing of information marked Confidential to counsel for similarly situated parties in pending litigation and that the notice needs to include the name of the counsel and a copy of the complaint in the other case subject to the following protections: 1) the recipient of such confidential information shall acknowledge in writing that he/she is not presently nor intends to be a competitor or employed by a competitor of any of the parties or counsel for any such person, and will not use any Confidential document, transcript or information obtained from any Confidential document or transcript to compete with any of the parties; and 2) plaintiff's counsel may not make such a disclosure without providing 30 days advance notification of the intent to make such disclosure to defendants' counsel and during that 30 day period defendants have not filed a motion for a protective order prohibiting the disclosure. This order will be effective three years or at the end of the case, whichever comes later. Parties to meet and confer regarding form of order. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/hk

Notice Of Plaintiffs Motion For Protective Order Governing Discovery?
Off Calendar Nov-15-2016 Continued To Dec-29-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. VALERIE N. ROSE OF THE LAW OFFICES OF WALKUP, MELODIA & SCHOENBERGER, TEL. NO. 1-415-981-7210 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. ROBERT TAITZ OF THE TYSON & MENDES LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 1-415-464-4935 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - DEFENDANTS DID NOT STIPULATE FOR THE JUDGE PRO TEM TO HEAR THE MATTER. PLAINTIFF AMANDA LAMB'S Motion For Protective Order Governing Discovery is continued to December 29, 2016 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 302. The Parties to report to the Judge Pro-tem by December 2 if they can resolve the issue. Judge: Pro-tem: Thomas Cohen; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not Reported. =302/jpt
